data.title: Orchid Island Capital's initial Q1 GAAP EPS surpasses consensus
data.description:
Orchid Island Capital (NYSE:ORC) estimated Wednesday its Q1 2025 GAAP earnings at $0.18 per share, including an estimated $0.17 per share of net realized and unrealized gains on RMBS and derivative instruments.

The preliminary EPS reading exceeds the $0.08 average analyst estimate and $0.07 in the prior quarter.

The REIT's estimated book value per share of $7.94 at March 31, 2025, down from $8.09 at Dec. 31, 2024.

Its estimated total return on equity for the was 2.6%.

Orchid (NYSE:ORC) shares edged up 0.6% in after-hours trading.

data.title: Constellation Brands Non-GAAP EPS of $2.63 beats by $0.36, revenue of $2.16B beats by $40M; issues outlook
data.description:
• Constellation Brands press release (NYSE:STZ): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $2.63 beats by $0.36.
  • Revenue of $2.16B (+0.9% Y/Y) beats by $40M.
  • Issues fiscal 2026 reported EPS outlook of $12.33 - $12.63 and comparable EPS outlook of $12.60 - $12.90, as well as operating cash flow target of $2.7 - $2.8 billion and free cash flow projection of $1.5 - $1.6 billion.
  • Issues EPS growth outlook for fiscal 2027 of mid-single digit to low-double digit and for fiscal 2028 of low-single digit to mid-single digit, as well as cumulative operating cash flow and free cash flow projections of over $6 billion and over $5 billion, respectively.
  • Declares quarterly cash dividend of $1.02 per share Class A Common Stock, an increase of 1%.
  • Announced separately a divestiture of remaining mainstream wine brands, and restructuring actions expected to generate over $200M in net annualized cost savings by fiscal 2028.
  • The company’s board also authorized the repurchase of up to $4 billion of its publicly traded common stock, expiring on February 29, 2028.

data.title: Constellation Brands issues soft outlook, divests mainstream wine business
data.description:
Shares of Constellation Brands (NYSE:STZ) are under modest selling pressure in late Wednesday trading as the company’s FY26 outlook – which reflects the potential impact from U.S. trade policy – disappointed Wall Street, overshadowing a beat on both profit and sales for the most recent quarter.

The parent company of Corona beer earned a profit of $2.63 per share, up from $2.30 a year ago and 36 cents better than expected. This was achieved on a small improvement in sales to $2.164B, topping estimates of $2.12B.

In the company’s beer business net sales were relatively unchanged year over year as decline in shipments was largely offset by pricing. Depletions declined approximately 1% largely driven by decreases in Modelo Especial, Corona Extra, and the Modelo Chelada brands of approximately 1%, 6%, and 3%, respectively.

Wine and spirits organic net sales increased over 11% driven by contractual distributor payments, favorable product mix and volume growth across the U.S. wholesale business, and higher volume growth from international markets, mainly driven by Canada.

Looking ahead to FY26, Constellation Brands expects earnings to be between $12.60 - $12.90 versus the consensus estimate of $13.94 per share, as well as operating cash flow target of $2.7 - $2.8 billion and free cash flow projection of $1.5 - $1.6 billion. For FY26, organic net sales are expected to be down 2% to up 1%.

For FY27 to FY28, the company lowered its sales growth outlook to flat to up 3% versus previous outlook of up 1% to up 3%.

The company also announced plans to divest its mainstream wine brands and related facilities from its wine and spirits portfolio through an agreement with The Wine Group.

“This transaction reflects our multi-year strategy to reconfigure our business, resulting in a portfolio of higher-end wine and craft spirits brands that are aligned to evolving consumer preferences and help bolster our competitive position,” said CEO Bill Newlands.

Brands to be divested to The Wine Group include Woodbridge, Meiomi, Robert Mondavi Private Selection, Cook’s, SIMI, and J. Rogét sparkling wine, along with associated inventory, facilities, and vineyards.

Additionally. the company said it will undertake a review of its organizational structuring. This review is anticipated to deliver net annualized cost savings in excess of $200M by FY28 and will be completed within FY26.

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Constellation Brands tops FQ4 consensus, 2026 EPS guidance misses expectations
Gabelli Multimedia Trust board approves "at the market" common stock offerings
data.description:
• The Gabelli Multimedia Trust (NYSE:GGT) has authorized the fund to conduct offerings of common stock in “at the market” transactions at prices above the net asset value, which would be accretive to the common shares.
  • The board of directors will continue to evaluate the fund’s capital structure, including the ability for shareholders to subscribe for additional common stock. The Fund has previously completed six rights offerings.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Trump Media shares notch best gains since January this year
data.description:
Shares in Trump Media (NASDAQ:DJT) closed with double-digit gains and rose as much as 22.4%, being among the top gainers within the communications services sector on Wednesday.

In a post on his social media platform Truth Social, Donald Trump said, "THIS IS A GREAT TIME TO BUY!!! DJT." The post came hours before the announcement of the 90-day pause on the U.S. reciprocal tariffs unveiled by him earlier this month.

Over 14.45M DJT shares have changed hands during the trading session, 2x its 3-month daily moving average. Short interest on the stock is 5.47%.

DJT shares have fallen 49% so far this year while the benchmark S&P index (SP500) was down 7% for the same duration.

Seeking Alpha analyst and Investing Group leader Daniel Jones said in his March 25 article that DJT stock remains a 'strong sell' due to significant overvaluation and deteriorating financials, including a 58.3% stock drop since April 2024.

"Despite recent ventures into financial services and platform updates, the lack of user metrics and substantial revenue streams makes future growth uncertain," Jones said.

DJT has no Wall Street analyst rating or Quant ratings from Seeking Alpha but a "STRONG SELL" rating from SA analysts on average. The company has a market cap of $3.67B.

data.title: Costco records another strong sales month amid tariff turmoil
data.description:
Costco Wholesale Corporation (NASDAQ:COST) reported comparable sales for the retail month of March were up 6.4%, including a 7.5% increase in the U.S., 4.1% rise in Canada, and 2.9% gain for other international markets. E-commerce comparable sales shot up 16.2% during the quarter.

After stripping out the impact of foreign exchange and gas prices, comparable sales were up 9.1%, including an increase of 8.7% in the U.S.

Total sales for the five-week period that ended April 6 were up 8.6% to $25.51 billion.

The retailer noted that March had one additional shopping day versus last year, due to the calendar shift of Easter. The calendar effect positively impacted total and comparable sales by approximately one and one-half percent. Some analysts have speculated that Costco (NASDAQ:COST) may have seen extra traffic during the month from consumers looking to load up on household products ahead of higher prices due to a trade war.

Costco (COST) currently operates 903 warehouses, including 623 in the United States and Puerto Rico, 109 in Canada, 41 in Mexico, 36 in Japan, 29 in the United Kingdom, 19 in Korea, 15 in Australia, 14 in Taiwan, seven in China, five in Spain, two in France, and one each in Iceland, New Zealand and Sweden. Costco also operates e-commerce sites in the U.S., Canada, the U.K., Mexico, Korea, Taiwan, Japan and Australia.

Shares of Costco (COST) rose 0.7% in postmarket trading to $971.50 after rallying 6.2% during the regular session. On a year-to-date basis, Costco (COST) is up 5.3%.

data.title: US Steel sinks after Trump says 'special company' should not go to Japan
data.description:
US Steel (NYSE:X) -15.2% post-market Wednesday after President Trump said he does not want to see the company taken over by Japan.

"We love Japan but US Steel is a very special company," Trump reportedly said.

US Steel (NYSE:X) shares had reached a 52-week intraday high $46.18 during the regular trading session, a day after Trump ordered a national security review of its planned sale to Nippon Steel (OTCPK:NPSCY) (OTCPK:NISTF).

More to come...

data.title: National Health Investors stockholder urges boardroom change
data.description:
Land & Buildings Investment Management, a significant shareholder of National Health Investors (NYSE:NHI) filed a proxy naming its nominees for the healthcare REIT's board on Wednesday, saying they would "instill true independence" on the company's board and help maximize shareholder value.

In a letter to shareholders, Land & Buildings, which is controlled by Jonathan Litt, said that NHI's stock is underperforming its peers due to "an archaic corporate governance structure" and a board that's "rife with conflicts of interest."

It pointed out that the company only committed to declassify its staggered board after Land & Building engaged with the company. Also, it highlighted multiple board members' significant stock ownership in one of NHI's (NYSE:NHI) largest tenants, National Healthcare Corp.

Instead of engaging with Land & Buildings, "the company has chosen instead to make reactive and one-sided changes that we believe fall well short of what is needed to fix the broken governance at NHI."

The shareholder is urging NHI stockholders to vote for its nominees — Adam Troso and Jim Hoffmann.

NHI (NHI) share barely moved in Wednesday after-hours trading.

data.title: Venus Concept plummets on $1.1M registered direct offering
data.description:
• Venus Concept (NASDAQ:VERO) said on Wednesday that it has entered into definitive agreements for the purchase and sale of 328,573 shares of common stock at a purchase price of $3.50 per share in a registered direct offering priced at-the-market under Nasdaq rules. 
  • The gross proceeds from the offering are expected to be approximately $1.1M, before deducting placement agent fees and other offering expenses payable by the company. 
  • The company intends to use the net proceeds from the offering for general corporate purposes.
  • Venus Concept (NASDAQ:VERO) shares were down more than 47% in extended trading.
